perturbation.original_text: ‘Confidential Information’ shall mean: in respect of Information provided in documentary or by way of a model or in other tangible form, Information which at the time of provision is marked or otherwise designated to show expressly or by necessary implication that it is imparted in confidence; and in respect of Information that is imparted orally, any information that the Disclosing Party or its representatives informed the Receiving Party at the time of disclosure was imparted in confidence; and in respect of Confidential Information imparted orally, any note or record of the disclosure and any evaluation materials prepared by the Receiving Party that incorporate any Confidential Information; and any copy of any of the foregoing; and the fact that discussions are taking place between the Receiving Party and the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.changed_text: ‘Confidential Information’ shall mean: in respect of Information provided in documentary or by way of a model or in other tangible form, Information which at the time of provision is marked or otherwise designated to show expressly or by necessary implication that it is imparted in confidence; and in respect of Information that is imparted orally, any information that the Disclosing Party or its representatives informed the Receiving Party at the time of disclosure was imparted in confidence; and in respect of Confidential Information imparted orally, any note or record of the disclosure and any evaluation materials prepared by the Receiving Party that incorporate any Confidential Information; and any copy of any of the foregoing. The fact that discussions are taking place between the Receiving Party and the Disclosing Party is explicitly excluded from the definition of Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text creates an in-text contradiction by explicitly excluding the fact that discussions are taking place from the definition of 'Confidential Information.' This contradicts the original text, which includes this fact. This creates uncertainty regarding whether the discussions themselves are protected, potentially impacting other sections related to non-disclosure of confidential matters related to the meetings.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Confidentiality obligations In consideration of the Disclosing Party providing Confidential Information, at its discretion , to the Receiving Party, the Receiving Party shall: Keep the Confidential Information secret and confidential; Neither disclose nor permit the disclosure of any Confidential Information to any person, except for disclosure to Authorised Persons in accordance with clause 3, or to a court or other public body in accordance with clause 4; Not use the Confidential Infor mation for any purpose, whether commercial or non commercial, other than the Permitted Purpose; [Make only such limited number of copies of the Confidential Information as are required for the Permitted Purpose, and provide those copies only to Authorised Persons];] and Take proper and all reasonable measures to ensure the confidentiality of the Confidential Information. For the purposes of this Agreement, the following words shall have the following meanings: ‘Information’ shall include information wheth er of a technical, commercial or any other nature whatsoever provided directly or indirectly by the Disclosing Party to the Receiving Party in oral or documentary form or by way of models, biological or chemical materials or other tangible form or by dem onstrations and whether before, on or after the date of this Agreement. ‘Confidential Information’ shall mean: in respect of Information provided in documentary or by way of a model or in other tangible form, Information which at the time of provision is marked or otherwise designated to show expressly or by necessary implication that it is imparted in confidence; and in respect of Information that is imparted orally, any information that the Disclosing Party or its representatives informed the Receiving Party at the time of disclosure was imparted in confidence; and in respect of Confidential Information imparted orally, any note or record of the disclosure and any evaluation materials prepared by the Receiving Party that incorporate any Confidential Information; and any copy of any of the foregoing. The fact that discussions are taking place between the Receiving Party and the Disclosing Party is explicitly excluded from the definition of Confidential Information; and the fact that discussions are taking place between the Receiving Party and the Disclosing Party. ‘Disclosing Party’ shall mean the party to this Agreement that discloses Information, directly or indirectly to the Receiving Party under or in anticipation of this Agreement. ‘Receiving Party’ shall mean the party to this Agreement that receives Information, directly or indirectly from the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly excludes 'the fact that discussions are taking place between the Receiving Party and the Disclosing Party' from the definition of Confidential Information. The contradicted text includes this statement in the definition of Confidential Information, and it later references the definition in a section about Confidentiality obligations. This creates a direct conflict within the document as to whether those discussions are covered under the confidentiality clause, and thus a strong in-text contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party may disclose the Confidential Information to those of its officers, employees (together, ‘Authorised Persons’) who : reasonably need to receive the Confidential Information to enable the Receiving Party to achieve the Permitted Purpose; have been informed by the Receiving Party (a) of the confidential nature of the Confidential Information and (b) that the Disclosing Party provided the Confidential Information to the Receiving Party subject to the provisions of a written confidentiality agreement
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party may disclose the Confidential Information to those of its officers, employees (together, ‘Authorised Persons’) who : reasonably need to receive the Confidential Information to enable the Receiving Party to achieve the Permitted Purpose; have been informed by the Receiving Party (a) of the confidential nature of the Confidential Information, but (b) are not required to know that the Disclosing Party provided the Confidential Information to the Receiving Party subject to the provisions of a written confidentiality agreement
perturbation.explanation: By removing the requirement for authorized persons to be informed that the disclosing party provided confidential information subject to a written agreement, the clause introduces a contradiction. It is now unclear whether authorized persons are bound by the original confidentiality agreement, creating an in-text contradiction with the rest of Section 3, especially the part that says 'have written confidentiality obligations to the Receiving P arty that (a) are no less onerous than the provisions of this Agreement'. It weakens the confidentiality protection and introduces potential disputes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Disclosure to employees The Receiving Party may disclose the Confidential Information to those of its officers, employees (together, ‘Authorised Persons’) who : reasonably need to receive the Confidential Information to enable the Receiving Party to achieve the Permitted Purpose; have been informed by the Receiving Party (a) of the confidential nature of the Confidential Information, but (b) are not required to know that the Disclosing Party provided the Confidential Information to the Receiving Party subject to the provisions of a written confidentiality agreement; in the case of the Receiving Party’s officers and employees, have written confidentiality obligations to the Receiving P arty that (a) are no less onerous than the provisions of this Agreement and (b) apply to the Confidential Information, and who have been instructed to treat the Confidential Information as confidential; have been provided with a copy of this Agreement and have agreed with the Receiving Party in writing to comply with the obligations of the Receiving Party under this Agreement, and that agreement provides that the Disclosing Party will be entitled to enforce the agreement as a third -party beneficiary; a nd in the case of the Receiving Party’s solicitors, have confirmed that they will treat the Confidential Information as if it were the Receiving Party’s confidential information and therefore subject to the rules of the Law Society concerning client infor mation. The Receiving Party shall be responsible for taking reasonable action to ensure that its Authorised Persons comply with the Receiving Party’s obligations under this Agreement and shall be liable to the Disclosing Party for any breach of this Agree ment by such Authorised Persons.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the original clause to remove the requirement that authorized persons be informed that the disclosing party provided confidential information subject to a written agreement. The contradicted text repeats the changed text and therefore also removes the requirement. This leads to a contradiction because it makes the obligations of the authorized persons less clear, creating an in-text contradiction with the intention of the original agreement to maintain confidentiality as explained in the Perturbation Explanation.

perturbation.original_text: Return and provide to the Disclosing Party all documents and other materials that contain any of the Confidential Information, including all copies made by the Receiving Party representatives; Permanently del ete all electronic copies of Confidential Information from the Receiving Party’s computer systems; and Provide to the Disclosing Party a certificate, signed by an officer of the Receiving Party, confirming that the obligations referred to in clause 6.1 h as been met.
perturbation.changed_text: Return and provide to the Disclosing Party only those documents and other materials that contain trade secrets, excluding all other Confidential Information, including all copies made by the Receiving Party representatives; Permanently del ete all electronic copies of Confidential Information from the Receiving Party’s computer systems; and Provide to the Disclosing Party a certificate, signed by an officer of the Receiving Party, confirming that only the obligations to return trade secrets referred to in clause 6.1 h as been met.
perturbation.explanation: Limiting the return of documents to only those containing 'trade secrets' contradicts the broader definition of 'Confidential Information' established earlier in the agreement (Section 2). This creates an in-text contradiction, as the Receiving Party is only obligated to return a subset of the information that is defined as confidential. It introduces potential disputes over what constitutes a trade secret vs. other confidential information and whether all confidential information must be returned, affecting the return of information obligations and protection afforded to the disclosing party. The certificate is also now changed to reflecting to trade secrets
perturbation.contradicted_text: Subject to clause 6.2, the Receiving Party shall (a) at the Disclosing Party’s request, and al so (b) upon any termination of this Agreement: Return and provide to the Disclosing Party only those documents and other materials that contain trade secrets, excluding all other Confidential Information, including all copies made by the Receiving Party representatives; Permanently del ete all electronic copies of Confidential Information from the Receiving Party’s computer systems; and Provide to the Disclosing Party a certificate, signed by an officer of the Receiving Party, confirming that only the obligations to return trade secrets referred to in clause 6.1 h as been met.
perturbation.justification: The changed text limits the return of documents to only those containing 'trade secrets,' excluding other 'Confidential Information,' which directly contradicts the original broader obligation to return 'all documents and other materials that contain any of the Confidential Information.' This creates a clear inconsistency regarding what information needs to be returned, thus it adheres to the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition. The change in the certificate requirement also reflects this contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: As an exception to its obligations under clause 6.1, the Receiving Party may retain one copy of the Confidential Information, in paper form, in the Receiving Party’s legal files for the purpose of ensuring compliance with the Receiving Party’s obligations under this Agreement. Following the date of any termination of this Agreement, or any return of Confidential information to the Disclosing Party (‘Final Date’), (a) the Receiving Party shall make no further use of the Confidential In formation, and (b) the Receiving Party’s obligations under this Agreement shall otherwise continue in force without limit of time.
perturbation.changed_text: As an exception to its obligations under clause 6.1, the Receiving Party may retain unlimited copies of the Confidential Information, in any form, in the Receiving Party’s files for any purpose. Following the date of any termination of this Agreement, or any return of Confidential information to the Disclosing Party (‘Final Date’), (a) the Receiving Party shall make no further use of the Confidential In formation, and (b) the Receiving Party’s obligations under this Agreement shall otherwise continue in force without limit of time.
perturbation.explanation: The change from 'one copy...in paper form...for the purpose of ensuring compliance' to 'unlimited copies...in any form...for any purpose' creates a significant in-text contradiction. It undermines the core confidentiality obligations established earlier in the agreement (Section 2) by allowing the Receiving Party to retain and potentially use the Confidential Information without restriction after termination. The contradiction is evident because it goes against the intent of the NDA to protect confidential information. This change introduces potential disputes concerning the use and protection of confidential information after the agreement's termination.
perturbation.contradicted_text: As an exception to its obligations under clause 6.1, the Receiving Party may retain unlimited copies of the Confidential Information, in any form, in the Receiving Party’s files for any purpose. Following the date of any termination of this Agreement, or any return of Confidential information to the Disclosing Party (‘Final Date’), (a) the Receiving Party shall make no further use of the Confidential In formation, and (b) the Receiving Party’s obligations under this Agreement shall otherwise continue in force without limit of time.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the original text from allowing 'one copy...in paper form...for the purpose of ensuring compliance' to 'unlimited copies...in any form...for any purpose'. This change contradicts the purpose of an NDA to protect confidential information and maintain restrictions on its use, especially after termination. The contradicted text which is the same as the Changed Text creates a direct conflict by allowing the Receiving Party to retain and potentially use the Confidential Information without restriction after termination, while also stating that the Receiving Party shall make no further use of the Confidential Information and that their obligations continue without limit of time, creating a direct and evident inconsistency based on the explanation provided and definition 5 (Inconsistencies).

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement does not include, expressly or by implication, any representations, warranties or othe r obligations: To grant the Receiving Party any licence or rights other than as may be expressly stated In this Agreement; To require the Disclosing Party to disclose, co ntinue disclosing or update any Confidential Information;To require the Disclosing Party to negotiate or continue negotiating with the Receiving Party with respect to any further agreement and either party may withdraw from such negotiations at any time without liability; nor As to the accuracy, efficacy, complet eness, capabilities, safety or any other qualities Whatsoever of any information or materials provided under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement does include, expressly or by implication, the obligation: To grant the Receiving Party a perpetual, irrevocable, worldwide license to use any Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement. This Agreement does not include, expressly or by implication, any representations, warranties or othe r obligations: To require the Disclosing Party to disclose, co ntinue disclosing or update any Confidential Information;To require the Disclosing Party to negotiate or continue negotiating with the Receiving Party with respect to any further agreement and either party may withdraw from such negotiations at any time without liability; nor As to the accuracy, efficacy, complet eness, capabilities, safety or any other qualities Whatsoever of any information or materials provided under this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The added clause 'This Agreement does include, expressly or by implication, the obligation: To grant the Receiving Party a perpetual, irrevocable, worldwide license to use any Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement' directly contradicts the first sentence: 'This Agreement does not include, expressly or by implication, any representations, warranties or other obligations' and Section 7 'all property, including intellectual property, in Confidentia l Information disclosed to it by the Disclosing Party shall remain with and be vested in the Disclosing Party and/or the Disclosing Parties Clients'. This creates a significant in-text contradiction concerning the ownership and usage rights of the Confidential Information. This conflict introduces considerable legal uncertainty and potential disputes about intellectual property rights.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement does include, expressly or by implication, the obligation: To grant the Receiving Party a perpetual, irrevocable, worldwide license to use any Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement. This Agreement does not include, expressly or by implication, any representations, warranties or othe r obligations: To require the Disclosing Party to disclose, co ntinue disclosing or update any Confidential Information;To require the Disclosing Party to negotiate or continue negotiating with the Receiving Party with respect to any further agreement and either party may withdraw from such negotiations at any time without liability; nor As to the accuracy, efficacy, complet eness, capabilities, safety or any other qualities Whatsoever of any information or materials provided under this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a clause that grants a perpetual, irrevocable, worldwide license to the Receiving Party, which directly contradicts the original statement that the agreement does not include obligations to grant such licenses. The added clause in the changed text is also in the same location as the contradicted text, strengthening the fact of an in-text contradiction. Therefore, a strong in-text contradiction exists.
